input.system:

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

samples.output: <think>The rear panel of the black computer chassis displays the installed power supply unit at the bottom, featuring a power socket, switch, and honeycomb grill labeled 'RM750i'. A hand holds the lower right corner of the case. The wooden desk surface and internal cables are visible nearby.</think><answer>From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.</answer>

metadata.gold_answer: From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.
metadata.canonical_answer: At 4s, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface; at 10s, a person places the black motherboard on its box; at 14s, the processor is installed into the socket; at 18s, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case.

input.system:
System: memory token budget exceeded. Compress the specified thinks into a summary.

Output: <summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>

Rules: retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR text, numbers, and state changes.

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] The video opens with a close-up of a computer interior featuring a white fan, black braided cooling tubes, and a GIGABYTE motherboard, before cutting to a black screen with white particles and a small white jersey graphic labeled "12". [2-6] The graphic expands into a white silhouette of a figure flexing arms with "SONCK 12" on the jersey back, shifting left as the text "SONCKI" appears to the right, followed by a horizontal line underneath. [6-10] The scene transitions to a display of computer component boxes including a Corsair H100i v2, RM750i power supply, MSI H270 PC MATE motherboard, Intel Core i5 processor, Samsung 850 EVO SSD, Corsair Vengeance RAM, and a Gigabyte G1 Gaming box showing "GEFORCE GTX 1060". [10-12] A person is shown assembling the hardware, with hands working on the MSI motherboard resting on its box, near an Intel Core i5 box, blue-handled pliers, and screws.

[14-18] An individual installs a processor into the CPU socket of an MSI motherboard, using both hands to align it before securing the black retention arm. A blue "CORE i5" box, white paper, and blue-handled pliers sit on the wooden table nearby. [18-22] The scene transitions to the interior of a black computer case with honeycomb mesh panels and a white fan. The motherboard is now mounted inside. The person uses both hands to press down on the board near the CPU socket and expansion slots, ensuring it is firmly seated within the chassis. [22-26] Using a screwdriver, first silver then with a green handle, the person secures the motherboard to the case standoffs. They tighten screws near the top right and bottom edges of the board. Bundled black cables run along the lower section of the case interior as the installation is completed.

[26-28] Inside the black computer case, a person uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots, tightening a screw to fix the board in place while the camera shows the left side of the chassis and the metal expansion slot covers. [28-30] The camera angle adjusts to display the fully installed motherboard within the black chassis, where white metal expansion slot covers line the rear left side and an MSI motherboard box with "H270" text is visible in the background. [30-32] A hand holding the green-handled screwdriver tightens a screw near the CPU socket on the MSI motherboard, revealing the silver heatsink labeled "PRO SERIES" clearly below the socket while the CPU socket remains empty in the center. [32-34] A person uses a knife to slice the tape on a black and yellow box labeled "RM 750i" and "Corsair," which rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis, as hands manipulate the blade and box flap to unseal the power supply packaging.

[38-42] A person positions a black and yellow Corsair box (OCR: "RM 750i") vertically near an open black computer case. A silver multi-tool and green screwdriver rest on the wooden table. The outer packaging is set aside, leaving a plain brown cardboard box lying flat; the person opens the top flap displaying the "CORSAIR" logo in black ink.
[42-46] The brown box opens to reveal white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside. The individual removes a white manual listing "RM 1000i", "RM 850i", "RM 750i", and "RM 650i". Finally, the black power supply unit is lifted out of the packaging, leaving cables inside the box next to the chassis.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The person's hands focus on the center of the MSI motherboard, manipulating the open CPU socket with its retention arm raised. They handle a small component directly over the processor area. The Intel Core i5 box and blue-handled pliers remain on the wooden desk to the right.

[34-36] A person uses a silver knife to cut the tape on a black and yellow box labeled 'RM 750i' and 'CORSAIR'. The box rests on a wooden table beside the open black computer chassis. Hands manipulate the packaging, pulling at the plastic wrap to unseal the power supply unit.

[36-38] The individual rotates the black and yellow Corsair power supply box, displaying the rear panel with technical specifications and diagrams. The silver knife rests on the wooden table surface. Both hands grip the packaging next to the open black computer case structure.

[46-48] After setting aside a white manual, the individual lifts the black power supply unit, encased in a protective bag, out of the brown cardboard box. Bundled cables remain visible inside the packaging on the wooden table, situated next to the open black computer chassis.

[48-50] The individual holds a black power supply unit wrapped in clear plastic in their right hand and a black fabric bag in their left hand. The open brown cardboard box rests on the wooden table, positioned next to the black computer chassis structure on the right side of the frame.

[50-52] The person holds a black power supply unit with both hands, revealing the white "CORSAIR" logo and "RM750i" text on the side panel. The protective plastic wrapping is gone, and a black fabric bag with a white logo lies on the table near the open computer case frame.

[52-54] The black power supply unit is now mounted inside the rear bottom of the black computer chassis. The exterior back panel reveals the power socket, switch, and 'RM750i' text on the fan grill. A hand touches the rear of the case near the installed unit, confirming its placement.

[54-56] The rear panel of the black computer chassis displays the installed power supply unit at the bottom, featuring a power socket, switch, and honeycomb grill labeled 'RM750i'. A hand holds the lower right corner of the case. The wooden desk surface and internal cables are visible nearby.

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

Large white fan blades occupy the left foreground, and vertical 'DDR4 BOOST' text appears on the right side as the person works.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.

White drive bays are visible on the left, and the Corsair power supply unit is located at the bottom.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe each step of the PC assembly process as it happens in the video.

answers.text: At 4 seconds, component boxes are arranged on a wooden surface. By 10 seconds, a person places the black motherboard onto its box. At 14 seconds, the processor is carefully installed into the CPU socket using both hands. Finally, at 18 seconds, the motherboard is lowered into the black computer case, where it is subsequently secured with a screwdriver near the PCIe expansion slots.

From 26 to 32 seconds, the assembly continues inside the black chassis. A green-handled screwdriver secures the motherboard near the PCIe expansion slots and CPU socket area. The camera reveals white metal expansion slot covers lining the rear left, while the MSI motherboard box remains visible in the background. Although the heatsink is visible, the CPU socket appears empty in the final frames as the board is fully fixed in place.

From 34 to 42 seconds, the focus shifts to unboxing the power supply. A silver knife cuts tape on a black and yellow Corsair RM 750i box. The individual rotates the package to show specifications before lifting it vertically. Finally, the inner brown cardboard box is opened, revealing white instruction manuals and black cables nestled inside, while the assembled chassis stands nearby.

From 46 to 50 seconds, the individual removes the power supply unit from its packaging, taking it out of a protective bag while bundled cables remain in the box. By 50 seconds, the plastic wrapping is discarded, revealing the black unit with the white Corsair logo. Finally, between 52 and 54 seconds, the power supply is shown fully mounted inside the rear bottom of the chassis, with a hand confirming its secure placement near the fan grill.

From 58 to 62 seconds, a hand uses a green-handled screwdriver to secure a screw into the top left mounting hole of the power supply. Between 70 and 76 seconds, the focus shifts inside the chassis where hands manipulate black cable connectors above the CPU socket. The person routes these bundled wires along the right interior wall near white chassis markings, connecting them to the upper edge of the installed MSI motherboard.
